initial_program:
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Tilemap transformation with a wall row of colour 5
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
total_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
total_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

%  Wall row (full row of a nonzero colour)
wall_row(W), wall_val(Vw) :-
    total_cols(M),
    #count{C : input(W,C,Vw)} = M,
    Vw != 0.

%  Candidate source cells (nonwall, nonzero, not the wall colour)
candidate(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != wall_val, R != wall_row.

%  Distance of a candidate to the wall
candidate_dist_above(R,C,V,D) :-
    candidate(R,C,V), R >= wall_row, D = R - wall_row.
candidate_dist_below(R,C,V,D) :-
    candidate(R,C,V), R < wall_row, D = wall_row - R.

candidate_dist(R,C,V,D) :-
    candidate_dist_above(R,C,V,D).
candidate_dist(R,C,V,D) :-
    candidate_dist_below(R,C,V,D).

%  Minimal distance for each column
col_min_dist(C,D) :-
    candidate_dist(R,C,V,D),
    #count{R2,C2,V2,D2 : candidate_dist(R2,C2,V2,D2), D2 < D } = 0.

%  Maximal colour for that minimal distance
col_max_colour(C,V) :-
    col_min_dist(C,D), candidate_dist(R,C,V,D),
    #count{R2,C2,V2,D2 : candidate_dist(R2,C2,V2,D2), D2 = D, V2 > V } = 0.

%  Global minimal distance and maximal colour among those columns
global_min_dist(G) :-
    col_min_dist(C,G),
    #count{C2,D2 : col_min_dist(C2,D2), D2 < G } = 0.

max_colour_G(Vmax) :-
    global_min_dist(G), col_max_colour(C,Vmax),
    #count{C2,V2 : col_max_colour(C2,V2), col_min_dist(C2,G), V2 > Vmax } = 0.

%  The source cell for each column
source(C,R,V) :-
    col_min_dist(C,D), col_max_colour(C,V), candidate_dist(R,C,V,D).

%  Columns that are HIGH (distance = G and colour = Vmax)
is_high(C,R,V) :-
    source(C,R,V), global_min_dist(G), max_colour_G(Vmax), V = Vmax,
    R >= wall_row, D = R - wall_row, G = D.
is_high(C,R,V) :-
    source(C,R,V), global_min_dist(G), max_colour_G(Vmax), V = Vmax,
    R < wall_row, D = wall_row - R, G = D.

%  Cells filled between source and wall (full interval)
high_fill(R,C,Vmax) :-
    is_high(C,Rsource,Vmax), Rsource < wall_row, R >= Rsource+1, R <= wall_row-1,
    input(R,C,0).
high_fill(R,C,Vmax) :-
    is_high(C,Rsource,Vmax), Rsource > wall_row, R >= wall_row+1, R <= Rsource-1,
    input(R,C,0).

%  One step outward from a nonhigh source (low / other columns)
outward(R,C,Vsrc) :-
    source(C,Rsource,Vsrc), not is_high(C,Rsource,Vsrc), Rsource < wall_row,
    R = Rsource-1, R >= 0, input(R,C,0).
outward(R,C,Vsrc) :-
    source(C,Rsource,Vsrc), not is_high(C,Rsource,Vsrc), Rsource > wall_row,
    R = Rsource+1, R <= max_row, input(R,C,0).

%  Helper predicates
max_row(Rmax) :- total_rows(N), Rmax = N - 1.
source_cell(R,C,V) :- source(C,R,V).
wall_cell(R,C,Vw) :- R = wall_row, input(R,C,Vw).
original_nonzero(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    not source_cell(R,C,V), not wall_cell(R,C,_),
    not high_fill(R,C,_), not outward(R,C,_).
zero_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,0), not wall_cell(R,C,_), not high_fill(R,C,_),
    not outward(R,C,_), not source_cell(R,C,_).

%  Output: exactly one colour per cell
output(R,C,Color) :- true.
output_cell(R,C) :- true.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

%  Enforce the correct colour by forbidding any other colour
%  (the solver will be forced to choose the only allowed colour)
:- wall_cell(R,C,Vw), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vw.
:- high_fill(R,C,Vmax), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vmax.
:- outward(R,C,Vsrc), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vsrc.
:- source_cell(R,C,Vsrc), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vsrc.
:- original_nonzero(R,C,Vorig), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vorig.
:- zero_cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

% -------------------------------------------------
%  Tilemap transformation with a wall row of colour 5
% -------------------------------------------------
%  Grid dimensions
total_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R,_,_)}.
total_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

%  Wall row (full row of a nonzero colour)
wall_row(W), wall_val(Vw) :-
    total_cols(M),
    #count{C : input(W,C,Vw)} = M,
    Vw != 0.

%  Candidate source cells (nonwall, nonzero, not the wall colour)
candidate(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != wall_val, R != wall_row.

%  Distance of a candidate to the wall
candidate_dist_above(R,C,V,D) :-
    candidate(R,C,V), R >= wall_row, D = R - wall_row.
candidate_dist_below(R,C,V,D) :-
    candidate(R,C,V), R < wall_row, D = wall_row - R.

candidate_dist(R,C,V,D) :-
    candidate_dist_above(R,C,V,D).
candidate_dist(R,C,V,D) :-
    candidate_dist_below(R,C,V,D).

%  Minimal distance for each column
col_min_dist(C,D) :-
    candidate_dist(R,C,V,D),
    #count{R2,C2,V2,D2 : candidate_dist(R2,C2,V2,D2), D2 < D } = 0.

%  Maximal colour for that minimal distance
col_max_colour(C,V) :-
    col_min_dist(C,D), candidate_dist(R,C,V,D),
    #count{R2,C2,V2,D2 : candidate_dist(R2,C2,V2,D2), D2 = D, V2 > V } = 0.

%  Global minimal distance and maximal colour among those columns
global_min_dist(G) :-
    col_min_dist(C,G),
    #count{C2,D2 : col_min_dist(C2,D2), D2 < G } = 0.

max_colour_G(Vmax) :-
    global_min_dist(G), col_max_colour(C,Vmax),
    #count{C2,V2 : col_max_colour(C2,V2), col_min_dist(C2,G), V2 > Vmax } = 0.

%  The source cell for each column
source(C,R,V) :-
    col_min_dist(C,D), col_max_colour(C,V), candidate_dist(R,C,V,D).

%  Columns that are HIGH (distance = G and colour = Vmax)
is_high(C,R,V) :-
    source(C,R,V), global_min_dist(G), max_colour_G(Vmax), V = Vmax,
    R >= wall_row, D = R - wall_row, G = D.
is_high(C,R,V) :-
    source(C,R,V), global_min_dist(G), max_colour_G(Vmax), V = Vmax,
    R < wall_row, D = wall_row - R, G = D.

%  Cells filled between source and wall (full interval)
high_fill(R,C,Vmax) :-
    is_high(C,Rsource,Vmax), Rsource < wall_row, R >= Rsource+1, R <= wall_row-1,
    input(R,C,0).
high_fill(R,C,Vmax) :-
    is_high(C,Rsource,Vmax), Rsource > wall_row, R >= wall_row+1, R <= Rsource-1,
    input(R,C,0).

%  One step outward from a nonhigh source (low / other columns)
outward(R,C,Vsrc) :-
    source(C,Rsource,Vsrc), not is_high(C,Rsource,Vsrc), Rsource < wall_row,
    R = Rsource-1, R >= 0, input(R,C,0).
outward(R,C,Vsrc) :-
    source(C,Rsource,Vsrc), not is_high(C,Rsource,Vsrc), Rsource > wall_row,
    R = Rsource+1, R <= max_row, input(R,C,0).

%  Helper predicates
max_row(Rmax) :- total_rows(N), Rmax = N - 1.
source_cell(R,C,V) :- source(C,R,V).
wall_cell(R,C,Vw) :- R = wall_row, input(R,C,Vw).
original_nonzero(R,C,V) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != 0,
    not source_cell(R,C,V), not wall_cell(R,C,_),
    not high_fill(R,C,_), not outward(R,C,_).
zero_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,0), not wall_cell(R,C,_), not high_fill(R,C,_),
    not outward(R,C,_), not source_cell(R,C,_).

%  Output: exactly one colour per cell
output(R,C,Color) :- true.
output_cell(R,C) :- true.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

%  Enforce the correct colour by forbidding any other colour
%  (the solver will be forced to choose the only allowed colour)
:- wall_cell(R,C,Vw), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vw.
:- high_fill(R,C,Vmax), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vmax.
:- outward(R,C,Vsrc), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vsrc.
:- source_cell(R,C,Vsrc), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vsrc.
:- original_nonzero(R,C,Vorig), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vorig.
:- zero_cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.

output_cell(R,C) :- true.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

%  Enforce the correct colour by forbidding any other colour
%  (the solver will be forced to choose the only allowed colour)
:- wall_cell(R,C,Vw), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vw.
:- high_fill(R,C,Vmax), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vmax.
:- outward(R,C,Vsrc), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vsrc.
:- source_cell(R,C,Vsrc), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vsrc.
:- original_nonzero(R,C,Vorig), output(R,C,Color), Color != Vorig.
:- zero_cell(R,C), output(R,C,Color), Color != 0.
